descriptions.text: Get ready to crumble! Once again, the clay fighters prove that when you play a state-of-the-art fighter, you might lose lives, you might lose limbs, but there's no reason to lose your sense of humor! Fight in and out of 3D backgrounds. Get tossed out of a window and resume fighting outside. Choose form 12 eccentric fighters including brand new combatants and old favorites like "Bad Mister Frosty" and "Bonker the Clown." Includes bonus "secret characters" who are so famous that a legal gag order prohibits us from revealing their identity. Move freely in over 12 highly detailed 3D environments.

ClayFighter 63⅓ is a 1997 fighting game developed and published by Interplay Productions for the Nintendo 64. It is the third installment in the ClayFighter series. The title is a parody of the 64 suffix common in Nintendo 64 games, and a reference to Naked Gun 33 1/3.
Upon release, ClayFighter 63⅓ was negatively received by critics for its gameplay, animation, and AI. An updated version, titled ClayFighter: Sculptor's Cut, was released in 1998 as a Blockbuster rental exclusive. Due to its limited distribution, Sculptor's Cut is one of the rarest and most valuable Nintendo 64 games.
